avalancheActivity.highlights: In the afternoon as the precipitation becomes more intense there will be a rapid increase in the avalanche danger to level 4 (high).
avalancheActivity.comment:
A lot of snow will fall. As the snowfall becomes more intense the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. In the afternoon over a wide area danger level 4 (high) will be reached.
In all aspects more frequent large avalanches are to be expected as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
Avalanches can reach valley bottoms and in some places endanger exposed transportation routes.

Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in ski touring, freeriding and snowshoe hiking.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
A lot of snow will fall until Sunday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The southerly wind will transport the new snow significantly.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights:
A lot of snow will fall until Sunday.
The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack in all aspects.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach quite a large size. Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches, including large ones.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: From midday as the precipitation becomes more intense there will be a gradual increase in the avalanche danger to level 4 (high). The backcountry touring conditions are precarious.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Saturday: A lot of snow will fall. As the snowfall becomes more intense the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow significantly. The new snow of Saturday is bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
In particular on steep shady slopes more frequent occasionally large avalanches are to be expected in particular from starting zones at higher altitudes. In the typical avalanche paths in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the dry avalanches can in some cases reach as far as the valley bottom.
It is inadvisable to engage in ski touring, freeriding and snowshoe hiking.

snowpackStructure.comment:
40 to 100 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Saturday above approximately 1600 m. Towards its surface, the snowpack is unfavourably layered. The new snow of Saturday is bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
In some regions 5 to 10 cm of snow fell on Thursday above approximately 2200 m.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Snow that is very compacted on the surface and stabilised by the stormy winds at the end of February, or composed of faceted crystals and, locally, snow with weak cohesion and surface frost. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

tendency.highlights: Over a wide area new snow: The fresh snow as well as the sometimes large wind slabs that are forming over a wide area represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Up to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last few days above approximately 1800 m. Above approximately 1300 m snow will fall from midday. The wind will be strong over a wide area.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from southerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form in the afternoon in particular at intermediate and high altitudes. In all aspects and above approximately 1500 m medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are to be expected as the snowfall becomes more intense.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for great caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow, and up to 30 cm in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m. This snow is poorly bonded with the old snowpack in some places.
Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until Sunday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The southerly wind will transport the new snow significantly.

Over a wide area there is still a very large amount of snow.

tendency.highlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: From midday as the precipitation becomes more intense there will be a gradual increase in the avalanche danger. The backcountry touring conditions are to some extent precarious.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Saturday: As the snowfall becomes more intense the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. The moderate wind will transport the new snow. The new snow of Saturday is bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Especially on steep shady slopes more frequent medium-sized to large avalanches are to be expected in particular from starting zones at higher altitudes. In the typical avalanche paths the dry avalanches can in some cases reach intermediate altitudes.
Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
30 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Saturday above approximately 1600 m. Towards its surface, the snowpack is unfavourably layered. The new snow of Saturday is bonding poorly with the old snowpack in some places.
In some regions 5 to 10 cm of snow fell on Thursday above approximately 2200 m.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Snow that is very compacted on the surface and stabilised by the stormy winds at the end of February, or composed of faceted crystals and, locally, snow with weak cohesion and surface frost. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

tendency.highlights: New snow: The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming over a wide area represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Above approximately 1300 m snow will fall from midday. The wind will be strong over a wide area.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from southerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form in the afternoon in particular at intermediate and high altitudes. In all aspects and above approximately 1500 m medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are to be expected as the snowfall becomes more intense.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for great caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Above approximately 1200 m snow will fall until Sunday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The southerly wind will transport the new snow significantly.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks a little snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Some fresh snow and the wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on northwest to north to northwest facing aspects above approximately 2800 m. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection. Shady slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious.
avalancheActivity.comment: Some fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released by large additional loads in particular above approximately 2400 m. This applies in particular in shady places that are protected from the wind. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in isolated cases. The old snowpack will be unstable in the north.

snowpackStructure.comment:
At elevated altitudes a treacherous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers, in particular by large additional loads.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Winter sport participants can release avalanches in isolated cases. These can reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
As the day progresses the likelihood of wet snow slides being released will increase a little below approximately 2000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2000 m: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes.
Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies on shady slopes.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.

5 to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Sunday. Fresh wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering on near-ridge shady slopes.
On very steep sunny slopes moist loose snow slides are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: Winter sport participants can release avalanches in isolated cases. These can reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2000 m: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes.
Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies on shady slopes.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

tendency.highlights:
Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.

10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Sunday. Fresh wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering on near-ridge shady slopes. In addition mostly small moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected, in the event of prolonged bright spells in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Winter sport participants can release avalanches in isolated cases. These can reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep west and east facing slopes below approximately 2200 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2000 m: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes.
Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals. This applies on shady slopes.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.

5 to 15 cm of snow will fall until Sunday. As a consequence of wind, mostly small wind slabs will form adjacent to ridgelines.
In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation moist loose snow slides are to be expected, in the event of solar radiation in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the precipitation. Towards the evening danger level 3 (considerable) will be reached.
avalancheActivity.comment: 15 to 30 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1400 m. This applies in particular towards the evening. The wind will be moderate. As a consequence of the precipitation the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. Moist and wet avalanches require caution. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area wind and new snow: Over a wide area the wind slabs will increase in size.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: 10 to 50 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1400 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding avalanches are the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack. These conditions brought about a gradual weakening of the snowpack also at intermediate and high altitudes. In some localities light snowfall above approximately 1800 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack, even by a single winter sport participant. They can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. Avalanche prone locations are to be found especially above approximately 2000 m. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

In the late morning the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep south and southwest facing slopes below the tree line.

As a consequence of warming during the day individual mostly small wet loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced over a wide area. Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

In some localities 2 to 5 cm of snow will fall in the late morning, especially above approximately 1800 m.

tendency.highlights: Over a wide area 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Sunday. The strong wind will transport the new snow. Fresh wind slabs are prone to triggering on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. In some localities up to 25 cm of snow will fall from late morning above approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. This applies in particular on northeast, north and northwest facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. On steep shady slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. The natural activity of avalanches will gradually increase.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack remains generally subject to considerable local variations. The rain will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1700 m. In some localities up to 25 cm of snow will fall above approximately 2000 m. Above approximately 2000 m northwest, north and northeast facing slopes: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Above approximately 1300 m snow will fall from midday. The wind will be strong over a wide area.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from southerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs will form in the afternoon in particular at intermediate and high altitudes. In all aspects small and medium-sized avalanches are to be expected as the snowfall becomes more intense.
The new snow is lying on top of a quite favourable old snowpack.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads especially at elevated altitudes. This applies in particular on extremely steep shady slopes above approximately 2700 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Above approximately 1200 m snow will fall until Sunday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The southerly wind will transport the new snow significantly.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks a little snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of the snowfall.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated dry avalanches are possible. They can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Some snow will fall.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
As a consequence of warming during the day individual mostly small wet loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies especially above the tree line.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced. The weather conditions during the course of the night gave rise to slight moistening of the snowpack.

In some localities 2 to 5 cm of snow will fall in the late morning, especially above approximately 1800 m.

tendency.highlights:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 1600 m. The strong wind will transport the new snow. Fresh wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
The avalanche danger will increase.
Over a wide area 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Sunday. The strong wind will transport the new snow. Fresh wind slabs are prone to triggering on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will increase.

avalancheActivity.comment: New snow is lying on a moist old snowpack. Above approximately 1400 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.
